response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack engineer with a strong testing culture and some genuinely relevant low-latency systems work, but he misses the role's core minimum qualifications by a wide margin. Professional C++ experience amounts to roughly a year (National Instruments plus a thesis project) against a required 8 years, and his large-scale infrastructure exposure (~2 years at Sociomantic, some Kubernetes and AWS CDK) falls well short of the 5-year bar. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang product work rather than cloud compute platforms, and he has no formal technical-leadership track record of owning cross-team strategy, priorities, and mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling, and teaching/workshop experience
Broad polyglot background across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded with cloud exposure (Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker)
concerns: Roughly 1 year of professional C++ versus the required 8 years; primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
Well under 5 years building large-scale infrastructure or distributed systems; most roles are product/web-focused
No formal technical-leadership role leading platform strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers; leadership evidence is limited to workshops and a small founder project
